page.content: The analysis of risk allele numbers reveals that for the row labeled 0-1, there were 84 cases (7.55%) and 208 controls (11.26%), with an adjusted odds ratio (OR) of 1.00 and a P value of —. For the row labeled 2-3, there were 482 cases (43.31%) and 848 controls (45.89%), with an adjusted OR of 1.40 (1.05-1.87) and a P value of 0.022.

page.content: In the row labeled 4-5, there were 456 cases (40.97%) and 705 controls (38.15%), resulting in an adjusted OR of 1.52 (1.14-2.04) and a P value of 4.63×10−3. The row labeled 6 showed 91 cases (8.18%) and 87 controls (4.71%), with an adjusted OR of 2.60 (1.73-3.90) and a P value of 3.91×10−6. The trend analysis indicates a P value of 2.83×10−5. Additionally, the variable analysis for insulin-requiring diabetes shows that for the row labeled No, the crude estimate (95% CI) is Reference with a P value of —, and for Yes, it is −0.9 (−1.9 to 0.09) with a P value of 0.07 and an adjusted estimate (95% CI) of −0.9 (−1.8 to −0.04) with a P value of 0.04. For creatinine level, per 10 μmol/l, the crude estimate (95% CI) is 0.05 (0.02 to 0.08) with a P value of 0.003, and the adjusted estimate (95% CI) is 0.05 (0.02 to 0.08) with a P value of 0.002.
